rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type annexes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model to avoid introducing undefined references.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees should reference component ports only when they are visible in the scope of the annex.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Ensure that all AGREE expressions use types and names that are explicitly declared or visible in the AADL model.
Avoid generating bounds or constraints that are not explicitly stated in the requirement or implied by the AADL data types.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Constants and equations in AGREE should be declared with appropriate types matching AADL port data types.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid in that scope.
Do not assume numeric bounds for ports unless explicitly stated in the requirement or compatible with the AADL data type.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax and structure in component implementations
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL components and features
